title: Quiz: Introduction to Kernel Methods
description: Test your understanding of kernel fundamentals

questions.question: What is the main advantage of the kernel trick?

options.text: It makes algorithms faster by using GPUs
It computes inner products in high-dimensional spaces without explicit feature computation
It reduces the number of training samples needed
It automatically selects the best features

questions.explanation: The kernel trick allows computing inner products ⟨φ(x), φ(y)⟩ in high-dimensional (even infinite) feature spaces without explicitly computing φ(x) and φ(y), which would be computationally expensive or impossible.

questions.question: What condition must a kernel function satisfy to be valid?

options.text: It must be bounded between 0 and 1

questions.explanation: A valid kernel must be positive definite, meaning the Gram matrix K_ij = K(x_i, x_j) has all non-negative eigenvalues for any finite set of data points.

questions.question: What does Mercer's theorem guarantee?

options.text: All kernels have finite-dimensional feature spaces
A feature map exists for any continuous, symmetric, positive definite kernel
The optimal kernel can be found automatically
Kernel methods always outperform deep learning

questions.explanation: Mercer's theorem states that for any continuous, symmetric, positive definite kernel K(x,y), there exists a feature map φ such that K(x,y) = ⟨φ(x), φ(y)⟩.

questions.question: What is the feature space dimension for the RBF (Gaussian) kernel?
questions.explanation: The RBF kernel K(x,y) = exp(-γ||x-y||²) corresponds to an infinite-dimensional feature space, which is why the kernel trick is essential—we could never compute the explicit features.

questions.question: The sum of two valid kernels is always a valid kernel.
questions.explanation: True. If K₁ and K₂ are positive definite kernels, then K = K₁ + K₂ is also positive definite. This is one of the kernel combination rules.

questions.question: Which kernel would be most appropriate for data with complex, non-linear decision boundaries?
questions.explanation: The RBF kernel can model complex non-linear decision boundaries because it corresponds to an infinite-dimensional feature space. It's the most commonly used kernel for general non-linear problems.

questions.question: What is a major limitation of kernel methods for large datasets?

options.text: The Gram matrix requires O(n²) storage
They only work with numerical features

questions.explanation: Kernel methods require computing and storing the Gram matrix K_ij = K(x_i, x_j), which needs O(n²) storage and O(n²) or O(n³) time. This becomes prohibitive for large n.

questions.question: Which of the following are valid kernel combination operations? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Sum, product, and exponential of valid kernels produce valid kernels. However, K₁ - K₂ is NOT guaranteed to be valid as it may produce negative eigenvalues in the Gram matrix.
